title: Quesnelia arvensis
intro: Quesnelia arvensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae, commonly known as a terrestrial bromeliad. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Quesnelia arvensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Quesnelia arvensis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Quesnelia arvensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. This family is commonly known as the pineapple family, which consists of plants such as pineapples and air plants. The order Poales is a part of the subclass Commelinidae, which also includes other flowering plants such as orchids and daylilies. The subclass Commelinidae is part of the class Liliopsida, which includes various types of flowering plants with flowers that are typically in threes.
